texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Using the Ebola Risk Triage Tool
texts.text: Using the Ebola Risk Triage Tool

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The VA-Ebola Risk Triage Tool is intended for use by U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) clinical staff to evaluate patients identified as having traveled to an area where Ebola Virus Disease is prevalent or having had exposure to a known Ebola patient or their body fluids within the previous 21 days. It follows the Center for Disease Control (CDC)
texts.text: The VA-Ebola Risk Triage Tool is intended for use by U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) clinical staff to evaluate patients identified as having traveled to an area where Ebola Virus Disease is prevalent or having had exposure to a known Ebola patient or their body fluids within the previous 21 days. It follows the Center for Disease Control (CDC)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: A CPRS template was created that follows the CDC algorithm in guiding assessment of at-risk patients and recommending appropriate follow-up and actions based on the findings and risk level identified. The approved template, sponsored by the
texts.text: A CPRS template was created that follows the CDC algorithm in guiding assessment of at-risk patients and recommending appropriate follow-up and actions based on the findings and risk level identified. The approved template, sponsored by the

texts.label: text
texts.orig: , is intended for use throughout VA assessing these at-risk patients.
texts.text: , is intended for use throughout VA assessing these at-risk patients.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Initial identification of patients with travel or contact risks may be done through several different pathways, including telephone hotlines, public service announcements, and signage at the medical center,  all of which   will direct patients to the appropriate intake location as established by the facility. Patient check-in kiosks in clinic areas will have a screening question about travel to affected areas, and patients who enter a positive response will be immediately directed to clinic staff. Clinic check-in staff may also   ask the initial questions about travel or contact and will also immediately direct any patient with a positive response to clinical staff in compliance with locally established protocols.
texts.text: Initial identification of patients with travel or contact risks may be done through several different pathways, including telephone hotlines, public service announcements, and signage at the medical center,  all of which   will direct patients to the appropriate intake location as established by the facility. Patient check-in kiosks in clinic areas will have a screening question about travel to affected areas, and patients who enter a positive response will be immediately directed to clinic staff. Clinic check-in staff may also   ask the initial questions about travel or contact and will also immediately direct any patient with a positive response to clinical staff in compliance with locally established protocols.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The VA Ebola Risk Triage Tool is intended for use by clinicians who assess patients referred through any of these pathways.
texts.text: The VA Ebola Risk Triage Tool is intended for use by clinicians who assess patients referred through any of these pathways.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: How to document Ebola risk in CPRS
texts.text: How to document Ebola risk in CPRS

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Before documenting Ebola Risk, contact your facility’s Health Informatics Specialist/Clinical Applications Coordinator for the note title you should use and the location of the Ebola Risk Triage Tool under Shared Templates.
texts.text: Before documenting Ebola Risk, contact your facility’s Health Informatics Specialist/Clinical Applications Coordinator for the note title you should use and the location of the Ebola Risk Triage Tool under Shared Templates.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Access CPRS and select your patient.
texts.text: Access CPRS and select your patient.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Select New Note (ensure that you have a location displaying in the visit box).
texts.text: Select New Note (ensure that you have a location displaying in the visit box).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Select the note title that your facility is using to document Ebola Risk.
texts.text: Select the note title that your facility is using to document Ebola Risk.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Select the Template box and open Shared Templates.
texts.text: Select the Template box and open Shared Templates.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Scroll to Ebola Risk Triage Tool and select the template.
texts.text: Scroll to Ebola Risk Triage Tool and select the template.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Once it opens complete the necessary information.
texts.text: Once it opens complete the necessary information.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Review and electronically sign your note.
texts.text: Review and electronically sign your note.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If you have issues documenting Ebola Risk, contact your facility’s Health Informatics Specialist/Clinical Applications Coordinator or Infection Control POC.
texts.text: If you have issues documenting Ebola Risk, contact your facility’s Health Informatics Specialist/Clinical Applications Coordinator or Infection Control POC.
